rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 5, 1985 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Joseph Corey And Production Of Documents [Ccp Sec. 1987.1]; And Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. The Motion to Compel Attendance and Testimony of Joseph Corey and Production of Documents is GRANTED. The untimeliness argument is rejected as incorrect. It is undisputed that Respondent Brian Williams is co-trustee of the Williams Family Trust dated February 5, 1985. As co-trustee, he is entitled to documents and testimony from the attorney who represented the trust during trust formation, amendment and administration. Accordingly, attorney Joseph Corey is ordered to comply with the Subpoena dated December 15, 2021 (Exhibit A to Motion). As co-trustee, Respondent Brian Williams is a party who is a client within the attorney-client privilege with respect to communications regarding trust affairs. Therefore, attorney Corey shall not assert the attorney-client privilege as to co-trustee Brian Williams with respect to questions and documents regarding trust formation, amendment and administration. If attorney Corey is asked questions that relate to an attorney-client relationship other than that between the Trust and attorney Corey, he may decline to answer, citing privilege. If responsive documents are protected by an attorney-client relationship other than that between the Trust and attorney Corey, he must produce a privilege log identifying the documents. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the scheduling of the deposition. The deposition is to be held within 30 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. All requests for sanctions are DENIED. Respondent shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above, prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day befpre the scheduled hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JASON JESSUINY MEDINA DURON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF Limited ConservATOR OF PERSON
rulings.Rulings: Appearances: Jubilee Menzies appeared by zoom for and with Karla Duron (proposed conservator) by zoom. Dennis Mesina (court appointed counsel) appeared by zoom for and with Jason Medina Duron (proposed conservatee) by zoom. The court explained the petition as well as the rights to make decisions and powers that will be given to the conservator. Jason Duron stated that he understands and that he gives his consent. Mr. Mesina stated that he spoke with his client last week and informed him of his right to object, his right to a jury trial as well as all of the powers that his mother will have as the conservator, and that his client indicated that he understood, that he did not have any objections and that he agreed to the petition. The court noted that the Golden Gate Regional Center agreed with four of the five powers, but it did not agree on the fifth power about education. Mr. Mesina stated that he went over the fifth power with his client. The court granted the Petition for the Appointment of Karla Duron as the Limited Conservator of the Person of Jason Jessuiny Medina Duron with all five of the requested powers (order signed in open court). Further orders: 1. A compliance status hearing for filing of the required documents as reflected in the attachment to the order is set for October 3, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. 2. The assessment fee of $800.00 is waived. 3. A status report is to be filed one week before the hearing date of August 24, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. 4. The conservator shall obtain the conservatorship handbook from the court's website as reflected on the attachment to the order. Judge: Ross C. Moody. Court Investigator: Naomi Brandes. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Hagler. Not reported.
